subject: Sociology
title: Simmel on Individual Religiosity
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Simmel on Individual Religiosity.

hook.text: Some deeply spiritual people never attend a service. Georg Simmel wondered what that meant.

buildup.text: Simmel split religion as institution from religiosity as personal inner experience and yearning.

discovery.text: Religiosity is a basic human impulse — a need for meaning that exists before any organized church.

twist.text: This means religion doesn't create the impulse. It captures and channels a feeling already within us.

climax.text: Simmel predicted modern spirituality: people seeking meaning outside churches, on their own terms.

quiz.question: What did Simmel distinguish between?
options: Religion as institution and personal religiosity
